atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain in the left hand, particularly with gripping and golfing.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient states he's had this for a long time, approximately six months.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any recent injuries.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the lateral epicondyle of the left elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is full and without pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Grip strength is reduced on the left hand.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's symptoms are consistent with medial epicondylitis.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A cervical MRI is planned to rule out other causes of neck pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The doctor will discuss treatment options including physical therapy and activity modification.
